text: The optimal WBGT range for peak endurance running performance is 7.5°C–15°C, corresponding to approximately 10°C–17.5°C air temperature.
For every degree of WBGT outside the optimal 7.5°C–15°C range, endurance running performance declines by 0.3%–0.4%.
Air temperature is the single most important weather parameter affecting endurance running performance, with an importance score of 40% in machine learning models.
WBGT has greater predictive power for endurance running performance (R² = 0.11–0.47) than air temperature alone (R² = 0.04–0.34), because it integrates temperature, humidity, wind, and solar load.
specific_value: WBGT R²=0.11-0.47 vs air temp R²=0.04-0.34

text: More than one-quarter (26%) of endurance running events take place in moderate, high, or extreme heat conditions, and this proportion rises to approximately half when marathons are excluded.
All four weather parameters—temperature, humidity, wind speed, and solar load—should be evaluated together when planning race strategy and heat mitigation for endurance events.
